summary: The reproduction demonstrates that input {1..5..0} causes the Node.js process to hang indefinitely due to an infinite loop from zero increment, confirmed by timeout after 10 seconds.
expected_security_impact: The process should hang indefinitely when processing {1..5..0}, causing denial of service. The timeout mechanism detects this hang and confirms the vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: The Docker container was terminated after 10 seconds by the timeout command, indicating the process hung in an infinite loop and did not complete. This confirms the zero increment causes an infinite loop that prevents normal execution.
verification_method: The run.sh script uses timeout (10 seconds) to detect process hang. A properly functioning implementation would either reject the input with an error or complete quickly. The timeout exit code 124 confirms the process hung, distinguishing infinite loop from normal execution.
evidence_produced_by_run: Process timed out after 10 seconds (exit code 124)
Expected fast completion or error, but process hung indefinitely
Timeout confirms infinite loop in numeric sequence generation
Vulnerability triggers in expand('{1..5..0}') at lines 173 and 184

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the vulnerable v5.0.0 tag, and the Dockerfile mounts/copies it into the container.
